gate_approach: MSSWEAIE selected as gate guide (AI Engineering specialization, representative of pure graduate MSSWE format). All 4 guides parsed in same pass.

parser_changes.nature: general (applies to all guides, not family-specific)
parser_changes.why: All 3 pure swe_grad guides had 'Software Quality Assurance and Deployment' missing from AoS. The preceding course ('Software Architecture and Design') had a last bullet that ended without terminal punctuation. The parser's _is_bullet_continuation() function treated the course title 'Software Quality Assurance and Deployment' (80% Title Case, >30 chars) as a bullet continuation of the prior course. The next course's content was then lost.
parser_changes.fix: Added a Title Case ratio check before the len(line) > 30 continuation path. If ≥80% of the line's words start uppercase, the line is classified as a Title Case heading and NOT a continuation — returns False. This is a general safety guard that prevents Title Case course titles from being swallowed by preceding bullets that lack terminal punctuation.
parser_changes.regression_verified: Re-parsed BSCS, BSCSIA, MBA, MHA, MATELED, BSSESB, BSACC, MSCIN. All returned identical confidence levels and anomaly counts. No regressions detected.

guides.degree_title: Master of Science, Software Engineering - AI Engineering
Master of Science, Software Engineering - Domain Driven Design
Master of Science, Software Engineering - DevOps Engineering
Bachelor of Science, Software Engineering (BSSWE to MSSWE)
guides.medium_detail: SP has 'Scripting and Programming Foundations'; AoS has 'Scripting and Programming - Foundations' (with em-dash variant). Course IS present in AoS with complete description and competency bullets. The difference is a formatting variant between the SP table title and the AoS section title in the source PDF — both refer to the same course. 0 AoS anomalies, 38/38 courses present, 0 empty descriptions, 0 empty competency lists. MSSWEUG is a bridge guide covering the full BSSWE-to-MSSWE curriculum (38 courses).

structural_notes: All 3 pure graduate guides share identical structure: 10 SP rows, 2 AoS groups ([Software]=9, [Risk Management]=1), no capstone.
MSSWEUG is a BSSWE-to-MSSWE bridge/pathway guide covering 38 courses across 10 groups. Parser handles identically to the pure graduate guides — no branching needed.

The parser fix (_is_bullet_continuation Title Case guard) was required before the 3 pure graduate guides could reach HIGH confidence. Without the fix, 'Software Quality Assurance and Deployment' was lost from AoS in all 3 guides because the preceding bullet ('The learner justifies the software architecture used in a software system') ended without terminal punctuation.
After the fix, all 3 pure graduate guides parsed with 0 anomalies and 0 warnings.
MSSWEUG's MEDIUM is entirely due to a hyphen variant in one title ('Scripting and Programming Foundations' vs '...  - Foundations'). No data loss; the course is present and correct.
gate_result: PASS — 3/3 pure graduate guides HIGH confidence after parser fix. 1 MEDIUM (bridge guide, cosmetic title variant). 0 LOW.

recommendation.summary: 4/4 parsed. 3 HIGH / 1 MEDIUM / 0 LOW. Parser fix confirmed safe. Roll out full family.
recommendation.downstream_note: MSSWEUG: one course has SP title 'Scripting and Programming Foundations' vs AoS title 'Scripting and Programming - Foundations'. Same course; hyphen variant only. Safe to use AoS title in downstream.
